chemistry.reaction_name: Visible Light-Enabled Selective Depolymerization of Oxidized Lignin

substrates.substrate_A: Oxidized lignin model compounds (β-O-4 ketone linkages)

chemistry.product: Phenolic compounds and ketones (e.g., guaiacol and 4'-methoxyacetophenone)

mechanistic_details.key_intermediate: PDI•− anion (strongly reducing species, E1/2red = -2.14 V)

chemistry.scope_summary: Various oxidized β-O-4 lignin model compounds with different methoxy substitution patterns (H-, G-, and S-type units) underwent complete depolymerization. Real organosolv lignin from birch was depolymerized with 86% mass conversion to low-molecular-weight aromatic compounds.

optimization_table.key_findings: 5.0 mol% PDI photocatalyst with 9.0 equiv DIPEA and 9.0 equiv HCOOH gave >99% conversion and 99% yield

figures.caption: Figure 1.  Photograph of the photocatalytic continuous-flow set- up.

Scheme 1.  PDI photocatalytic depolymerization of oxidized β-O- 4 model molecule in continuous-flow reactor. | Figure 2.  2D HSQC NMR spectra (in CDCl 3 ) of extracted lignin (a),  Scheme of PDI photocatalytic depolymerization of authentic  lignin (b), GC-MS spectra of the depolymerization products (c).
Scheme 2.  Plausible mechanism of PDI photocatalytic  depolymerization lignin.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Long reaction time of 480 minutes required for complete conversion
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Dramatically reduced residence time to 1.25 minutes with higher yield
translation_logic.what_changed: Reactor design from batch to continuous flow micro-reactor; residence time reduced from 480 to 1.25 minutes
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Temperature (25°C), photocatalyst (PDI), additives (DIPEA, HCOOH), atmosphere (N2), solvent (MeCN)
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous flow operation with smaller volumes reduces hazard exposure

figure_descriptions: Scheme showing PDI photocatalytic depolymerization in flow reactor
NMR spectra and GC-MS data of lignin depolymerization products
Mechanistic scheme for PDI photocatalytic depolymerization

conflicts: Photocatalyst loading conflict: Pass 2 reports 2.5 mol% while optimization table mentions 5.0 mol% as optimal - used Pass 2 value for consistency with reported conditions
notes: Strong extraction confidence due to clear batch vs flow comparison with quantitative metrics. Time reduction factor of 384x calculated from 480 min batch to 1.25 min flow residence time. Yield improvement from 95% to 99% represents 4.2% increase. Some fields like flow rates and reactor volume not explicitly stated but overall experimental setup is well documented.
